title: Holography of the Loewner Energy
speaker: Yilin Wang
abstract: The Loewner energy is a Möbius invariant quantity that measures the roundness of Jordan curves on the Riemann sphere. It arises from large deviation deviations of SLE0+ and is also a Kähler potential on the Weil-Petersson Teichmüller space. Motivated by AdS/CFT correspondence and the fact that Möbius transformations extend to isometries of the hyperbolic 3-space H3 , we look for quantities defined geometrically in H3 which equal the Loewner energy of a curve in the conformal boundary. We show that the Loewner energy equals the renormalized volume of a submanifold of H3

proof_stages.label: Analytic formulations of Loewner energy
proof_stages.summary: The talk defines Loewner energy for a Jordan curve using Dirichlet integrals of conformal maps, recalls its independence of choices and PSL2(C)-invariance via determinant/anomaly identities, and records finite-energy characterizations by Weil–Petersson, Schwarzian, and Grunsky-type conditions.

proof_stages.label: Motivation for a hyperbolic-three-dimensional expression
proof_stages.summary: The Loewner energy is related to the universal Liouville action/Kähler potential, and since PSL2(C) is the isometry group of hyperbolic 3-space, earlier renormalized-volume phenomena suggest seeking a geometric formula in H3.

proof_stages.label: Limits of minimal-surface and convex-hull candidates
proof_stages.summary: Existing H3 objects associated to the boundary curve—minimal surfaces with finite renormalized area and convex hulls with L2 thickness—capture Weil–Petersson finiteness but do not naturally or uniquely compute Loewner energy itself.

proof_stages.label: Holographic W-volume theorem
proof_stages.summary: The main theorem states that for sufficiently smooth curves one quarter of the Loewner energy equals a renormalized volume between two canonical surfaces minus the integral of their mean curvature, with an inequality proved for general Weil–Petersson curves.

proof_stages.label: Epstein surfaces from visual metrics
proof_stages.summary: Using visual metrics and horospheres, the Epstein map sends a boundary conformal metric to the envelope of its horospheres; applying this construction to the hyperbolic metrics on the two complementary domains produces the two canonical surfaces.

proof_stages.label: Renormalization and boundary finiteness
proof_stages.summary: The circle case fixes the normalization, the two Epstein surfaces are shown to be well behaved near the ideal boundary so the truncated volume is defined, and curvature/area expressions are related explicitly to the Schwarzian to obtain finiteness criteria.

proof_stages.label: First-variation comparison and conclusion
proof_stages.summary: The proof concludes by matching the first variation of Loewner energy, given by the Schwarzian stress tensor, with the first variation of the renormalized volume functional, then using the base case for equality and smooth approximation plus lower semicontinuity for the Weil–Petersson inequality.

examples.name: Small-kappa SLE loop large deviations
examples.note: SLE loops illustrate one probabilistic origin of Loewner energy, where the energy is the large-deviation rate function as kappa tends to zero.
examples.engagement_reason: states the small-kappa SLE loop large-deviation relation with Loewner energy but does not derive or compute it.

examples.name: Square curve with corners
examples.note: The audience's square example illustrates the finiteness criterion: corners make the pre-Schwarzian behave like 1/z and hence fail the required L2 condition.
examples.engagement_reason: checks the square/corner case by noting the corner makes f''/f' behave like 1/z, hence not L2 and the energy is infinite.

passes.reason: A square is a highly symmetric polygon whose corner singularities are invoked to show finite Loewner energy fails.
